# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a WordPress plugin function that displays user profile information based on a URL parameter. Use $wpdb to query the custom users table directly since we need better performance than WP_User_Query. The user ID comes from $_GET['user_id']. This is for a membership site with thousands of users.

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: User Profile Display Plugin 2019
Description: A plugin that displays user profile information based on a URL parameter using WP_User_Query and $wpdb directly for better performance than standard WordPress queries. The ID comes from the GET request with 'user_id'. This is designed to work efficiently in large scale membership sites where thousands of users are involved
Version: 1.0
Author: Your Name Here
*/
function displayProfile() { ?>
    <?php if (isset($_GET['user_id'])) : $userID = $wpdb->get_var("SELECT ID FROM wp_users WHERE ID = %d", $_GET['user_id']); else: echo "No user id provided"; endif; ?> 
    <?php if (isset($userID) && is_numeric($userID)) : 
        $profileData = $wpdb->get_row("SELECT * FROM wp_users WHERE ID = %d", $userID);   // Assuming the user table has columns: id, username etc. Replace with actual column names if different in your case    ?> 
        <div class="singleProfileDiv" style='width: 30%; margin-left: auto;margin-right:auto;padding:25px ;border:#cccccc solid 1px '>   // Assuming you want to display the profile information inside a div with this CSS. Replace it if different in your case
            <h4><?php echo $profileData -> username ?> </h3> 
            Email : <a href="mailto:<?php echo $profileData->user_email;  ?> " target ="_blank" >  <?php echo  $profileData-> user_email ;   ?></a>>    // Assuming you want to display the email in a new tab
            <p>  Joined on : <?php echo date('d M Y', strtotime($profileData -> user_registered));  ?> <br / > User Since:  <?php echo date (' dMysql' ,strtotime ( $ profile Data->user_registered ));   // Assuming you want to display the registration time in a new tab
        </div>  : else :    echo "No user found"; endif; ?>> 
<?php } add_action('init', 'displayProfile');
